rulings.Case Title: HAROLD DOYLE et al VS. AMERICAN BILTRITE INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Strike Punitive Damages From Plaintiffsâ¿¿ Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 5, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT FRYER-KNOWLES, INC., MOTION To Strike Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs' Complaint. Motion to strike is denied. Punitive damages allegations are sufficient. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Toshia Websters And Defendant Tina Websters Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 5, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANT TINA WEBSTER Defendant Toshia Websters and Defendant Tina Websters Motion For Judgment on The Pleadings. Hearing required. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE et al VS. PEERLESS INDUSTRIES, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 5, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT PEERLESS INDUSTRIES, INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is overruled with ten days to answer. The fifth cause of action sufficiently alleges that plaintiffs have standing and there is an actual controversy. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PETER ROGOSIN VS. BAY AREA AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons On Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 5, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT BRIAN BUNGER Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On Second Amended Complaint. Bunger¿s motion to quash is granted. Plaintiff has not effected proper service on Bunger. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PETER ROGOSIN VS. BAY AREA AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT DISTRICT et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 5, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT LINDA TRIPOLI DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Tripoli¿s demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. All claims currently alleged against Tripoli are barred by the litigation privilege, but plaintiff is given an opportunity, if he is able, to allege claims against Tripoli not barred by the litigation privilege. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PETER ROGOSIN VS. BAY AREA AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT DISTRICT et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 5, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT BAY AREA AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT DISTRICT DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Bay Area Air Quality Management District¿s demurrer is overruled with ten days to answer. The second amended complaint is sufficiently intelligible to enable the District to respond and sufficiently alleges the necessary elements of each claim that is the subject of the general demurrer. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LUCIPA, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. AT&T CONSULTING SOLUTIONS, INC. A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 5, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANTS DAVID MINGO, AND, AT&T CONSULTING SOLUTIONS, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is denied. Alternate motion for summary adjudication granted as to the second, third and fourth causes of action and denied as to the first cause of action. There are triable issues whether the contract was at will and whether defendant intended to induce a breach or disrupt that contract. The economic advantage claims fail to the absence of any independently wrongful act. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JENNY S VASQUEZ VS. VICTOR GRANADOS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 5, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT HSBC BANK USA, N.A., MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Bank¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Bank¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the thirteenth cause of action and denied as to all other issues. Plaintiff does not seek restitutionary relief that is required for a UCL claim. Triable issues as to all other issues for which the Bank seeks summary adjudication. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JENNY S VASQUEZ VS. VICTOR GRANADOS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 5, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT CANTRELL, HARRIS & ASSOCIATES INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Cantrell¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Cantrell¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the third, sixth, seventh, eighth and thirteenth causes of action and denied as to all other issues. Plaintiff fails to establish a contractual relationship with Cantrell and does not seek restitutionary relief that is required for a UCL claim. Triable issues as to punitive damages and all claims not based on contract or the UCL. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HON WAH CHEUNG VS. SAM KOK NG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 5, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANT KOK NG MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Off calendar per the moving party. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 5, 2009, line 7, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. American Express. Off calendar. Case was dismissed on 8/24/09. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARIN MANAGEMENT, INC. VS. PRIME WEST DEVELOPMENT, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 5, 2009, line 11, DEFENDANT PRIME WEST DEVELOPMENT, LLC MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Motion to set aside the default and default judgment is denied. No proof of service or courtesy copies provided to this department. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer To Pltf'S Verified Complaint; Memorandum Ofp/A And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 5, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT ZOLILI NDLILI Demurrer To Plaintiff's Verified Complaint. Demurrer overruled with ten days leave to answer. Plaintiff sufficiently pleads a claim entitling him to relief. Special demurrer does not lie in a limited jurisdiction case. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARK DOWNING et al VS. LFG INVESTMENTS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 5, 2009, line 9, DEFENDANT JOHN REESE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. The alter ego allegations conflict with other allegations in the complaint. Plaintiff is given the opportunity to allege, if he is able to do so, sufficient alter ego liability for the breach of contract claim. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES JOHNSON VS. GLINDA M GREEN et al
